Definition: higher-level taxonomic/phylogenetic group as printed in the supplementary table
Note: Examples include monotreme, didelphimorphid, diprotodontid, primate, carnivore, etc.

Definition: species binomial as printed in the supplementary table (summary rows such as mean/SD retained where present)
Note: Includes specimen or summary labels exactly as printed, e.g. numbered specimens, mean, or SD rows.

Definition: common English name for the taxon as printed in the supplementary table
whole brain volume in cubic millimetres
Note: Used as the scaling variable for total cerebellar volume in the paper.

Definition: total volume of the corpus cerebelli in cubic millimetres
Note: Paper reports that corpus cerebelli volume scales approximately isometrically with brain volume.

Definition: volume of cerebellar vermal cortex excluding lobule 10 (nodulus), in cubic millimetres
Note: Corresponds to vermal lobules 1-9; used with hemisphere volume to assess hemispheric specialization.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Vermis (excl Cb10) (mm3)" and Table 1 description

Definition: volume of cerebellar hemisphere cortex excluding flocculus, in cubic millimetres
Note: Paraflocculus is included; this variable is used to quantify hemispheric expansion relative to vermis.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Hemisphere (excl Fl) (mm3)" and Table 1 description

Definition: volume of flocculonodular lobe cerebellar cortex (flocculus plus nodulus/lobule 10), in cubic millimetres
Note: Represents vestibulocerebellar cortex involved in vestibular/eye-head coordination.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Flocculo-nodular Cb Cx (mm3)" and Table 1 description

Definition: ratio of hemisphere cortex volume to vermal cortex volume
Note: Used in the paper as an index of cerebellar hemispheric enlargement or specialization.

Definition: total cerebellar cortical volume in cubic millimetres
Note: Represents total cerebellar cortex; used in scaling analyses of foliation, white matter, pontine nuclei, and deep cerebellar nuclei.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Total Cb Cx volume (mm3)"

Definition: volume of deep cerebellar white matter within the corpus cerebelli, excluding deep cerebellar nuclei, in cubic millimetres
Note: Used to assess internal connectivity of the cerebellum.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Cb white matter (mm3)" and Table 1 description

Definition: combined volume of basilar pontine nuclei and reticulotegmental pontine nuclei, in cubic millimetres
Note: The paper treats "pontine nuclei" here as including both basilar pontine and reticulotegmental nuclei.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Pn and RtTg volume (mm3)" and Results text

Definition: volume of deep cerebellar nuclei, including medial, intermediate and lateral nuclei plus interstitial cell group, in cubic millimetres
Note: Excludes nucleus Y and vestibulocerebellar nucleus, which are treated as part of the vestibular nuclear complex.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Deep Cb nu volume (mm3)" and Table 1 description

Definition: external surface area of the cerebellum in square millimetres, defined as the multi-sided convex polygon enclosing the cerebellar surface in contact with subarachnoid space or fourth ventricle lumen
Note: Used as the denominator in the foliation index.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Cb ext surface (ESA) (mm2)" and Table 1 description

Definition: total pial surface area of the cerebellum in square millimetres, including all sulci and fissures whether facing subarachnoid space or the fourth ventricle
Note: Used as the numerator in the foliation index.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Cb pial surface (PSA) (mm2)" and Table 1 description

Definition: foliation index calculated as pial surface area divided by external cerebellar surface area (PSC/ESC)
Note: Quantifies degree of cerebellar cortical folding.
Source Note: Supplementary table column "Foliation index" and Methods/Results text

Definition: source identifier for the dataset row
Note: Useful if harmonized with other extracted tables in the project.
Source Note: Suggested harmonization field analogous to the Barger and MacLeod definition files
